LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER IS FILED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER AND DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY OCTOBER 11, 2022. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-11-2024 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PER THE NOTICE OF THE DEMURRER, WHICH FRAMES THE ISSUES FOR THE MOTION, DEFENDANT DEMURRED TO THE ENTIRE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT INCLUDES 4 CAUSES OF ACTION. NEITHER THE DEMURRER NOR THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES CONTAIN ANY ARGUMENTS OR AUTHORITIES ADDRESSING ANY DEFICIENCIES WITH THE 4TH CAUSSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER INDICATES THAT IT IS ADDRESSING "THE FIRST AMENDED COMP LAINT," A PLEADING THAT IS NO LONGER OPERATIVE IN THIS ACTION, SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT HAVING BEEN FILED. BOTH THE NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND THE DEMURRER EXPRESSLY ARE BASED SOLELY ON FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE ANY CAUSE OF ACTION CITING TO CCP 430.10(E). THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES EXTENSIVELY DISCUS AND ARGUE UNCERTAINTY AND CCP 430.10(F), NOTICE OF SUCH ARGUMENT HAVING NOT BEEN PROVIDED IN THE NOTICE OF DEMURRER OR DEMURRER. COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY STATES CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE ARISING OUT OF PURPORTED MISHANDLING OF THE FEES PAID FOR FORECLOSURE SERVICES AND IDENTIFIES AT LEAST ONE FEE AS IMPROPERLY CHARGES AND WITHHELD, E.G. TRUSTEE FEES AT NOS. WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S UNDERSTANDING OF THE AGREEMENT REGARDING FORECLOSURE SERVICES, THE CHARGES AND THE TIMING WHEN THOSE CHARGES HAVE BEEN INCURRED IS CORRECT, IS NOT AN ISSUE THAT MAY BE ADDRESSED ON A DEMURRER. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF UNRUH ACT IS NOT SUPPORT BY CITATION TO ANY AUTHORITY AND THEREFORE, THE DEFENDANT FAILED TO CARRY THEIR BURDEN. FOR EXAMPLE, DEFENDANT FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR SUPPORT ITS ARGUMENT THAT KNOWLEDGE OF ETHNICITY OR AGE IS A REQUIRED ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION, ETC. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)
ORDER OVERRULING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF INTENGAN, ARDEN RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC. $16,270.00 PRINCIPAL, $4,268.22 ATTORNEY FEES, TOTAL JUDGMENT $20,538.22, SEE SCANNED DOC

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART, AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO NEGLIGENCE, UNRUH, AND BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIMS, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE ACCOUNTING CLAIM. THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM WAS UNSUPPORTED BY FACTS THAT ESTABLISH THE ELEMENT OF DUTY FOR NEGLIGENCE. THE UNRUH CLAIM WAS UNSUPPORTED BY FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION-PLAINTIFF ONLY MAKES CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS. THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM WAS UNSUPPORTED BY A COPY OF THE CONTRACT TO ITS PLEADING, AND THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DID NOT SPECIFY THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT. THE ACCOUNTING CLAIM IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT STATES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION. (CODE CIV. PROC., 430.10 SUBD. (E).)PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Apr-10-2024 Continued To Jun-05-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-29-2024 Continued To May-07-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Edward T. Weber; Law Offices of Edward T. Weber; 657-235-8359; appearing as counsel for defendant C.N.A. Foreclosure Services, Inc.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Per the Notice of the Demurrer, which frames the issues for the motion, Defendant demurred to the entire Second Amended Complaint. Second Amended Complaint includes 4 causes of action. Neither the Demurrer nor the Points and Authorities contain any arguments or authorities addressing any deficiencies with the 4th causse of action.
The Demurrer indicates that it is addressing "the First Amended Complaint," a pleading that is no longer operative in this action, Second Amended Complaint having been filed.
Both the Notice of Demurrer and the Demurrer expressly are based solely on failure to state facts sufficient to constitute any cause of action citing to CCP 430.10(e). The Points and Authorities extensively discus and argue uncertainty and CCP 430.10(f), notice of such argument having not been provided in the Notice of Demurrer or Demurrer.
Complaint sufficiently states causes of action for negligence arising out of purported mishandling of the fees paid for foreclosure services and identifies at least one fee as improperly charges and withheld, e.g. Trustee Fees at NOS. Whether Plaintiff's understanding of the agreement regarding foreclosure services, the charges and the timing when those charges have been incurred is correct, is not an issue that may be addressed on a demurrer.
Demurrer to the cause of action for violation of UNRUH Act is not support by citation to any authority and therefore, the defendant failed to carry their burden. For example, Defendant fails to cite any authority for support its argument that knowledge of ethnicity or age is a required element of the cause of action, etc.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC. DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained, with leave to amend, as to negligence, UNRUH, and breach of contract claims, and overruled as to the accounting claim. The Negligence claim was unsupported by facts that establish the element of duty for negligence. The UNRUH claim was unsupported by facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action-Plaintiff only makes conclusory allegations. The breach of contract claim was unsupported by a copy of the contract to its pleading, and the First Amended Complaint did not specify the terms of the contract. The accounting claim is overruled. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. (Code Civ. Proc., 430.10 subd. (e).)Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2023 Continued To Mar-17-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT C.N.A. FORECLOSURE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: continued to March 17, 2023 for the moving party to address the propriety of filing of an amended reply 16 days after the original hearing date. On January 9, 2023 the court continued the original hearing for the moving party to provide a courtesy copy of the reply on file at the time of the ruling (reply filed on December 28, 2022). The Court did not receive a courtesy copy of December 28, 2022 reply. On January 27, 2023 defendant filed an "amended reply," courtesy copy of which was received by the Court.

By no later than March 1, 2023 defendant to file a declaration (1) addressing why a courtesy copy of December 28, 2022 reply had not been delivered as ordered; and (2) provide authority for filing of an amended reply to be considered in lieu of the original reply. The Court notes that there is no explanation in the amended reply as to the reason for the amended filing or what parts of the reply are amended. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
